metadata.name: Cloudflared
metadata.description: Runs a Cloudflare Tunnel connector in Docker Compose so private services can be published through Cloudflare Zero Trust.
metadata.guide:
## Overview
Runs `cloudflared` as a Docker Compose service for Cloudflare Tunnel connectivity.

## Prerequisites
- A Cloudflare Zero Trust tunnel must already exist.
- Provide a valid tunnel token before starting the stack.
- The Docker network referenced by the template must already exist when you use an external network.

## Deploy
- Render `compose.yaml` and `.env`, review the network name and image tag, then start the stack with `docker compose up -d`.
- Use `docker compose logs -f` to verify the connector establishes a healthy tunnel session.

## Follow-Up
- Pair this with a tunnel ingress configuration such as `cloudflare-zero-trust-tunnel-config` so hostnames actually route traffic.
- Confirm the tunnel and connector both show healthy status in the Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard.

## References
- [Cloudflare Tunnel](https://developers.cloudflare.com/cloudflare-one/connections/connect-networks/)
- [Cloudflared Container](https://hub.docker.com/r/cloudflare/cloudflared)
